data.narrative:
QuEra Computing is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Mikhail Lukin, Vladan Vuletić, Markus Greiner, Dirk Englund, Nathan Gemelke, and John Pena. The analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Q-CTRL—operating across the quantum computing sector.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here and forms the primary triangulation anchor. Using QuEra's $230M in total raised as a base value proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $437M. The Peer-Median approach, while potentially valuable, cannot be reliably applied due to an insufficient narrow peer set: only one same-stage comparable exists within the quantum computing space, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold needed for defensible median analysis. Expanding to all-sector peers would introduce unwarranted noise.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $371M (low) to $503M (high), with a median of $437M. This single-method conclusion carries meaningful uncertainty around stage-specific growth assumptions and the heterogeneity of quantum modalities and go-to-market approaches within the peer set. Additional clarity on revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical differentiation would refine this range considerably.
